OEM and ODM Clothing Manufacturing in Pakistan

July 1, 2026 | By FABRIOZA

When brands contact clothing manufacturers, two terms constantly come up: OEM and ODM. Understanding the difference is critical — it affects your costs, timeline, intellectual property, and creative control. At FABRIOZA, we offer both OEM and ODM manufacturing from Pakistan with MOQ starting at 50 pieces.

What is OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturing)?

OEM means you provide the complete design, and we manufacture it to your exact specifications. You own the design 100%. We simply execute your vision.

Best for: Brands with unique designs, established tech packs, proprietary patterns, or specific construction requirements.

What you provide: Tech packs, sketches, reference garments, fabric specifications, sizing charts, and decoration details.

What FABRIOZA does: Pattern making, fabric sourcing, sample development, bulk production, quality control, and delivery.

What is ODM (Original Design Manufacturing)?

ODM means you select from our existing design templates and customize them with your branding. We provide the base design; you provide the brand.

Best for: Startups testing the market, brands launching quickly, or businesses wanting proven styles with their logo.

What FABRIOZA provides: Design templates, fabric options, sizing, construction methods.

What you customize: Colors, logos, labels, packaging, and minor design tweaks.

OEM vs ODM Comparison

FactorOEMODM
Design Ownership100% yoursAdapted from templates
Development CostHigher (custom patterns)Lower (existing designs)
TimelineLonger (pattern development)Faster (ready templates)
MOQ50 pieces50 pieces
ExclusivityFully exclusiveMay be adapted for others
Best ForUnique designsQuick market entry

When to Choose OEM

  • You have a unique silhouette or construction method
  • Your brand positioning demands 100% original designs
  • You're manufacturing patented or proprietary products
  • You have complete tech packs ready
  • Long-term brand differentiation is critical

When to Choose ODM

  • You're testing a new market or product category
  • Speed to market is more important than uniqueness
  • You want lower development costs
  • You're launching a basic/core collection
  • You need proven styles that already sell well

Can You Combine OEM and ODM?

Absolutely. Many successful brands use a hybrid approach: ODM for basic/core items (t-shirts, standard hoodies) and OEM for signature/hero pieces that differentiate their brand. This balances cost efficiency with brand uniqueness.

OEM or ODM — We Do Both

50 pieces minimum. Free consultation to determine the best approach for your brand.
